content.problemStatement:
Chef decided to redecorate his house, and now needs to decide between two different styles of interior design.

For the first style, tiling the floor will cost X1X_1X1​ rupees and painting the walls will cost Y1Y_1Y1​ rupees.

For the second style, tiling the floor will cost X2X_2X2​ rupees and painting the walls will cost Y2Y_2Y2​ rupees.

Chef will choose whichever style has the lower total cost. How much will Chef pay for his interior design?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing 444 space-separated integers X1,Y1,X2,Y2X_1, Y_1, X_2, Y_2X1​,Y1​,X2​,Y2​ as described in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the amount Chef will pay for interior design.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers X1 Y1 X2 Y2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum total cost, i.e. min(X1+Y1, X2+Y2).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
For each line we compute the two style costs and pick the smaller:
1) 10+20=30, 9+25=34 → 30
2) 10+20=30, 9+20=29 → 29
3) 10+20=30, 20+10=30 → both equal, answer 30
4) 100+43=143, 85+61=146 → 143

hidden.label: general case same total different distribution

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total cost of the first style as sum1 = X1 + Y1 and the total cost of the second style as sum2 = X2 + Y2. The answer is the smaller of the two sums. This requires only O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X1 = int(next(it)); Y1 = int(next(it)); X2 = int(next(it)); Y2 = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(min(X1+Y1, X2+Y2)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
